title: Formation Flight common median-phase boundary population source convergence

postRunAnalysisCorrection.reason: The independent audit showed that the first plotting pass computed the primary profile from reflectedIncoming + interpolationAuxiliary + movingWall instead of the preregistered exact production reconstructedIncoming value. The reconstruction closed at approximately 1e-7, so the difference was tiny but correctly failed the 1e-10 analysis-parity check.
postRunAnalysisCorrection.scope: Use rawReflectedPopulationSum + reconstructedIncomingPopulationSum for the primary and conditional profiles exactly as preregistered. Preserve the decomposed sums for component attribution and closure only.

scientificQuestion: At the deterministic common lattice offset nearest the c16/c18/c20 geometry medians, does the population-weighted near-wing boundary source follow the restored smooth geometry refinement trend?

rationale.why: The complete 192-pose geometry ensemble averaged out the stopped c18 bridge, but geometry-only counts cannot establish that population-weighted moving-boundary reconstruction behaves smoothly at a representative lattice phase.
rationale.roi: One coupled-only phase sample per grid reuses the five-cycle production trajectory and existing source census while avoiding three isolated controls, multiple phases, and a second full refinement ladder.
rationale.solverEvolution: Add an explicit common subcell translation to the coupled-only diagnostic path. The public production paths retain a zero-offset default and identical numerical behavior; the parent preregistered implementation remains exactly recoverable at the parent commit.

selectionRule.metric: sum over c16/c18/c20 of squared sample-SD-normalized distance from each resolution's leader areal-link-density median

decomposition.conditionalMomentumExchangePopulation: m_r(q) = (rawReflectedPopulationSum_r(q) + reconstructedIncomingPopulationSum_r(q)) / linkCount_r(q), with zero at zero support
decomposition.directionWeight: Euclidean length of each D3Q19 direction
decomposition.c18Expectation: linear interpolation of the c16 and c20 direction profiles at h18, where h = 1 / chordCells

nextActions.smoothPopulationWeightedSource: promote the common-offset source result to a minimal three-offset robustness check before rerunning a quantitative formation-power ladder
nextActions.populationWeightedSourceNonAsymptotic: use the preregistered reflected, interpolation, and moving-wall component curvatures to select one cell-resolved c18 trace without another full ladder
nextActions.mixedPopulationWeightedSource: run only the two next-best deterministic median-offset candidates to determine whether the residual is phase-local or systematic

claimBoundary: This one-phase three-grid experiment can determine whether the population-weighted boundary source follows the geometry trend at the selected representative common lattice phase. It cannot establish offset-ensemble robustness, quantitative formation-power convergence, a production solver promotion, or a biological bird-flight claim.
